stemHtml: On modern commercial aircraft, what is the the typical standard operating procedure regarding the use of autothrust on final approach in gusty and turbulent conditions?

options.a: Autothrust should be disconnected before final approach to prevent thrust oscillations.
options.b: Autothrust should remain engaged, unless it begins to react inappropriately to speed fluctuations.
options.c: Autothrust must remain off during all visual approaches in gusty conditions.
options.d: Autothrust should only be engaged below 500 feet in turbulent conditions.
